behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is model judgment quality on explicit imagery. A mocked classifier can validate routing, but it cannot reveal real misclassification behavior from the vision model.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of explicit images to the real vision model path and assert each is marked flagged and persisted in the human-review hold state, with no publish event emitted.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower-level tests only verify routing after classification, not whether real explicit images are actually detected.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about robustness of semantic detection under adversarial obfuscation, which is model-quality dependent and can vary with real model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants through the production text moderation model endpoint and assert they are flagged and held, including unicode lookalike and leetspeak variants.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-only tests with mocked labels would miss the real classification weakness this behavior targets.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The main risk is decision wiring across moderation results, hold logic, and publish path. This is a multi-module interaction failure, not model-quality validation.
behaviors.test_description: Using deterministic classifier stubs that return 'safe' for both image and text, execute submission through moderation service + persistence/queue modules and assert one publish record/event is created and no hold/drop state is recorded.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A pure unit test of a decision function would not catch silent drop failures in module handoff/persistence.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is end-to-end latency under real runtime conditions (network/model calls, queueing, service overhead). Mocked dependencies can hide true timeout/latency failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end latency test against deployed services with real model endpoints and realistic concurrent load; measure submit-to-decision time and assert p95 (or required SLA metric) is <= 5 seconds.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower levels cannot reliably represent real network/model latency and contention effects.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real failure handling at external-call boundaries (timeouts, connection errors, outage responses) plus retry queue interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-model unavailability (e.g., endpoint blackhole/503 in staging) and submit image posts; assert they enter retry queue with backoff metadata and are never auto-approved/published during outage window.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Mock exceptions can test code paths, but real outage modes often differ in timing/error shape and can break retry wiring.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This requires repeated agentic evaluations over time plus consistency controls (e.g., cache/idempotency/window policy). The failure emerges across a sequence, not a single call.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image N times within the configured short window through the full moderation flow using the real vision model; assert all decisions match and follow the intended stabilization rule (e.g., cached first decision).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Single-call agentic tests miss sequence/state consistency failures across repeated submissions.
